RE
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-07-023
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of City of port
Hueneme councilmember Jon sharkey regarding the conflict of interest privisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act').r The Commisiion's assistance is limited to matters
arising under the Act. (Regulation 18329(b)(8XD).) We urge you to check with the state
Attorney General's office to determine if there are other taw-s ttrat may be applicable in
light of the facts you present.
QUESTTON
May Councilmember Sharkey participate in govemmental decisions involving the
development of property located within 500 feet of a=property he previously owned but
recently exchanged with his mother for a different property in the sameiurisaiction that is
located more than 500 feet awat'l
CONCLUSION
Councilmember Sharkey is disqualified from participating in governmental
decisions involving the development of property locaied wiihin sbo feet of the property
he exchanged if such decisions will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial
effect on his economic interests.
FACTS
You are the city attorney for the City of Port Hueneme. Councilmember Sharkey
has asked that you request formal written advice from the Commission regarding the
following facts.

ANALYSIS
Your inquiry implicates the Act's conflict of interest rules, sections g7l00et seq
The Act stipulates that public officials "perform their duties in an impartial manner, free
from bias caused by their own financial interest, o. trr" nr*cial interest ofpersons who
have supported them'" (section 81001(b).) sp*in""[y a public official is prohibited
from making, participaring in mak-g o...olh:iir: ;r;; ii, o. her officiar position to
influence a govemmental decision in"which th" offi;i;i;^ u firrurrial interest. (Section
87100; regulation 1870o(a).) within the meaning of the Act, apublic official has a

Accordingly, by engagrng in any ofthese actions in his official capacity,
councilmember Sharkey will have made, participated in making used or attempted to
use his official position to influence a govemmental decision.
step Three: what are councilmember sharkeyrs Economic rnterests?
A public ofiEcial has a financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its
effect on the public generally, on the official, a member of his orher imirediate family,
or on any ofthe following economic interests:
o An economic interest in a business entity in which he or she has a direct or
indirect investrnent of $2,000 or more (Section 87 103(a);regulation I g703. I (a));
or in which he or she is a director, officer, partner, trusteg employee, or holds any
position of management. (section g7 I 03(d); reguiation r gzo:. r (b).1
o An economic interest in real property in which he or she has a direct or indirect
interest of $2,000 or more in faiimarket value. (Section szro:6U;; iegulation
18703.2, see section 92033 [defining..Interest in Rear propertr']i
o An economic interest in any source ofincome to him orher, which aggregates to
$500 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section AZf Of f");
regulation 18703.3.)
o An economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her, if the gifts aggregate to
$390 or more within 12 months prior tJthe decision. (Section s7103(e);
regulation 18703.4.)
o An economic interest in his or her personal finances, including those of his or her
immediate family. (section g7 to3;regulation l g703.5.)
Councilmember Sharkey has an economic interest in anyperson from whom he
has received income aggregatingto atleast $500 within 12 months prior to the time when
the relevant governmental decision is made. (Section g7103(c);regulation
18703'3(a)(1)') Section 82030 defines '1ncorne" broadly as "payment received.,, Section
82044 defines "pa5rment" as any "paTrment,hansfer, Ioan, advance, deposit, gin o. other
rendering of monelt, property, services or anything else oivalu", *h"th".turr1giut" o.
intangible-" (Emphasis added.)

step Five: Are the Financial Effects on councilmember sharkey's Economic
Interest Material?
A conflictof interest may arise only when the reasonably foreseeable impact of a
governmental decision on a public official's economic interests is material. (Regulation
18700(a)') Different standards apply to determine whether u ."^oouuiyfor"r""ubl"
financial effect on an economic interest will be materialdepending rn irr" outur" of the

agency's decision.
Regulation 18705.3 provides the materiality standard for an economic interest in
persons who are sources of income to councilmember sharkey. rrr" nr*.ial effect of a
governmental decision is deemed material with respect to an individual who is a source
of income to an offici al if arry of the following uppii"r,
"(A) The decision will affect the individual's incomq inveshnents, or other

Regulation 18705.2(b) provides that the financial effect of a governmental
decision on real property which is indirectly involved in the gor*ri"ntal decision is
presumed not tobe material- Thispresumption may be rebuttedbyproof that there are
specific circumstances regarding the govemmentaidecision, its financial effect, and the
nature of the real property in which the public official has an economic interest, which
make it reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on
the real property in which the official has an interest. Examples of these specific
circumstances include circumstances where the decision affects:
"(A) The. development potential or income producing potential of the real
property in which the official has an economic interest;
"(B) The use of the real property in which the official has an economic
interest;
"(c) The ghlqacter of the neigh-borhood including, but not limited to,
substantial effects on: traffic, view, pivacy,intensity ofusg noise levers, air
emissions, or similar traits of the neighborirood.,,

step Three: what are councilmember sharkeyrs Economic rnterests?
A public ofiEcial has a financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its
effect on the public generally, on the official, a member of his orher imirediate family,
or on any ofthe following economic interests:
o An economic interest in a business entity in which he or she has a direct or
indirect investrnent of $2,000 or more (Section 87 103(a);regulation I g703. I (a));
or in which he or she is a director, officer, partner, trusteg employee, or holds any
position of management. (section g7 I 03(d); reguiation r gzo:. r (b).1
o An economic interest in real property in which he or she has a direct or indirect
interest of $2,000 or more in faiimarket value. (Section szro:6U;; iegulation
18703.2, see section 92033 [defining..Interest in Rear propertr']i
o An economic interest in any source ofincome to him orher, which aggregates to
$500 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section AZf Of f");
regulation 18703.3.)
o An economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her, if the gifts aggregate to
$390 or more within 12 months prior tJthe decision. (Section s7103(e);
regulation 18703.4.)
o An economic interest in his or her personal finances, including those of his or her
immediate family. (section g7 to3;regulation l g703.5.)

QUESTION: May Councilmember Sharkey participate in govemmental decisions involving the
development of property located within 500 feet of a=property he previously owned but
recently exchanged with his mother for a different property in the sameiurisaiction that is
located more than 500 feet awat'l

CONCLUSION: Councilmember Sharkey is disqualified from participating in governmental
decisions involving the development of property locaied wiihin sbo feet of the property
he exchanged if such decisions will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial
effect on his economic interests.
